description: scapy.contrib.http is the HTTP layer for Scapy. Useful for HTTP-level packet crafting.

documentation.example: from scapy.all import IP, TCP, Raw; from scapy.layers.http import HTTP; print(HTTP())
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/scapy-http && python3 scapy-http.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/scapy-http && python3 scapy-http.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/scapy-http && python3 scapy-http.py --help`. Scapy's HTTP layer (built-in).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the web category. Fits when the engagement needs: Scapy's HTTP layer (built-in).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Scapy's HTTP layer (built-in).

metadata.example: from scapy.all import IP, TCP, Raw; from scapy.layers.http import HTTP; print(HTTP())

name: scapy-http
